project.title: Identification of novel constituents of ALIS from macrophage lysates (LPS+/-), LC MS/MS
project.projectDescription: Macrophages assemble aggresome-like induced structures or ALIS upon LPS or E. coli mediated TLR4 stimulation. These structures are positive for p62, LC3 and ubiquitinated proteins, form irrespective of autophagy, and persist within the cell system up to 48h. However, their composition is not well known. Our aim was to identify proteins constituting ALIS and establish its functionality in host-defense response under infectious conditions.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Insoluble lysate fractions from RAW264.7 macrophages, stimulated in the presence or absence of LPS, were resolved by ultracentrifugation on sucrose density gradient to obtain ALIS enriched fractions, verified by higher levels of p62 and LC3 proteins. The selected fractions, numbers 25 and 26, from LPS treated insoluble lysate and corresponding untreated insoluble lyste were resolved on SDS-PAGE and stained with Coomassie brilliant blue. Each of the lanes of the four fractions were excised and processed by in-gel digestion using trypsin. This was followed by micro-capillary LC/MS/MS analysis. Orbitrap LTQ Velos Pro mass spectrometer was used.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: To analyse the mass spectrometry data Proteome Discoverer (PD) 2.2 software was used. Data search was done using SequestHT against Mus musculus database from UniProt. Master proteins from the identified proteins were considered for screening. Proteins involved in defense response were identified.
